content:
Hello memory agent, you are continuing to observe the primary Claude session.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>修</user_request>
  <requested_at>2026-07-11</requested_at>
</observed_from_primary_session>

You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

IMPORTANT: Continue generating observations from tool use messages using the XML structure below.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

MEMORY PROCESSING CONTINUED
===========================

PROGRESS SUMMARY CHECKPOINT
===========================
Write progress notes of what was done, what was learned, and what's next. This is a checkpoint to capture progress so far. The session is ongoing - you may receive more requests and tool executions after this summary. Write "next_steps" as the current trajectory of work (what's actively being worked on or coming up next), not as post-session future work. Always write at least a minimal summary explaining current progress, even if work is still in early stages, so that users see a summary output tied to each request.

Claude's Full Response to User:
找到一個機制成立的根因候選，並且能解釋「1x 個、內容相同」的數量級。診斷報告如下：

## 事實整理（證據等級標注）

🟢 **A 級（trace/log 直接證實）**：
- Roadmap turn 是單一 session（`b43a6bb4`）、**單一 prompt、單一 end_turn**，275 個 chunk 組成一份 1501 字的答案——agent 沒有重講
- 1501 字 < 4096 → 單 chunk 送出路徑，排除分頁
- 只有一個 bridge process（10:16:47 起跑）→ 排除雙 poller
- 無 goal_step 事件 → 排除 goal continuation 迴圈
- `pendingMessages` queue 每項只重放一次、autoRetry 上限 3 → 都湊不出 10+ 次

∴ 重複發生在 **streaming edit 層**，不是 agent、不是 queue。

## 根因：`editNow` 沒有並發互斥（TOCTOU race）

🟢 機制在 code 裡直接成立（`run-prompt.ts:445-516, 550-562`）：

1. `onUpdate` 每個 chunk 觸發，`elapsed >= 1200ms` 就**直接呼叫 `editNow()`（fire-and-forget，無 in-flight 鎖）**
2. `lastEditAt` **只在成功送出後更新** → draft 送出一旦開始失敗，時間閘門對每個 chunk 全開（此 turn chunk 速率 ~10/秒）
3. 每個並發的 `editNow` 走到 draft 降級分支：`trySendDraft` 失敗（await #1）→ 檢查 `if (!placeholder)` → `await ctx.api.sendMessage(...)`（await #2）
4. **`placeholder` 要等第一個 `sendMessage` resolve 才被賦值**。在那 1-2 秒的網路延遲窗口內，後續每個並發呼叫檢查 `!placeholder` 都是 true → **每個都各自發出一則完整內容的新訊息**

重複數量 = 窗口內擠進來的並發呼叫數 ≈ 10 chunks/s × 1-1.5s ≈ **10-15 則**，和你看到的「1x 個」吻合；發生在 streaming 尾段時每則的 buffer 快照幾乎相同 →「內容都一樣」。

🟡 **B 級（推論）**：觸發點是 draft 送出在該時刻開始失敗（TTL 過期／你滑走清掉 draft／Telegram 瞬時錯誤）。哪一種無法回溯確認——bridge console 沒落檔。如果 start.bat 那個視窗還在，往回捲找 `10:17:4x` 附近的 log 可以定案。

## 附帶發現（同一區的第二個洞）

`editNow` 本身**不檢查 `turnFinalized`**——一個 in-flight 的 editNow 若在 final render 之後才 resolve，會再多冒出一則 streaming 幀訊息。skill 裡的 latch 只擋了 recovery listener，沒擋 editNow 自己。

## 建議修法（~12 行，單點）

`editNow` 加 in-flight latch + dirty flag（進行中就標記、結束後補跑一次最新內容），開頭加 `turnFinalized` 檢查：

```ts
let editInFlight = false, editPending = false;
const editNow = async () => {
  if (turnFinalized) return;
  if (editInFlight) { editPending = true; return; }
  editInFlight = true;
  try { /* 現有 body */ }
  finally {
    editInFlight = false;
    if (editPending) { editPending = false; void editNow(); }
  }
};
```

一個鎖同時解掉：placeholder 重複建立（本次事故）、draft 並發降級、final 被遲到幀覆蓋三個問題。要修嗎？

Respond in this XML format:
<summary>
  <request>[Short title capturing the user's request AND the substance of what was discussed/done]</request>
  <investigated>[What has been explored so far? What was examined?]</investigated>
  <learned>[What have you learned about how things work?]</learned>
  <completed>[What work has been completed so far? What has shipped or changed?]</completed>
  <next_steps>[What are you actively working on or planning to work on next in this session?]</next_steps>
  <notes>[Additional insights or observations about the current progress]</notes>
</summary>

IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this next PROGRESS SUMMARY - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the summary content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful summary content.

Thank you, this summary will be very useful for keeping track of our progress!

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

- The mechanism: `editNow` is called on every chunk when elapsed >= 1200ms, with no concurrency lock

- A fix is proposed: adding an in-flight latch + dirty flag pattern to prevent concurrent executions

content.text:
<summary>
  <request>Root cause analysis of duplicate message bug in Telegram bridge streaming layer</request>
  <investigated>Examined streaming edit mechanism in run-prompt.ts (lines 445-562), traced through session logs showing single turn (b43a6bb4) with 275 chunks, ruled out agent retries, queue replay, pagination, and goal continuation as causes. Analyzed timing patterns showing 10-15 duplicate messages with identical content appearing during streaming output.</investigated>
  <learned>The root cause is a TOCTOU race condition in `editNow()`: when draft sending fails (e.g., TTL expiry, Telegram error), each streaming chunk triggers `editNow()` without concurrency control. The `placeholder` variable is only set after the first `sendMessage()` resolves, creating a 1-1.5 second window where subsequent concurrent calls all see `!placeholder === true` and each sends a new complete message. Duplicate count (~10-15) matches chunk rate (10/s) × network delay window. Additional issue found: `editNow` doesn't check `turnFinalized`, allowing late-resolving calls to overwrite final messages.</learned>
  <completed>Completed full diagnostic report with evidence-graded findings (A-level: trace-confirmed, B-level: inferred). Identified exact code path and timing mechanism. Proposed fix using in-flight latch + dirty flag pattern (~12 lines) that would prevent: placeholder duplication, concurrent draft degradation, and final message overwrites.</completed>
  <next_steps>Awaiting user decision on whether to implement the proposed fix in run-prompt.ts editNow function.</next_steps>
  <notes>The diagnosis explains both the quantity (1x messages ≈ network delay window × chunk rate) and uniformity (all concurrent calls snapshot nearly identical buffer content at streaming tail). Bridge console logs from 10:17:4x timeframe would definitively confirm the trigger (draft failure type) if still available in start.bat window.</notes>
</summary>

<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Draft streaming test coverage examined</title>
  <subtitle>Smoke test validates plain/rich draft sending, placeholders, CRLF normalization, and API fallback</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Test file scripts/check-draft-streaming.mjs contains 150 lines covering trySendDraft functionality</fact>
    <fact>Test transpiles telegram-rich-renderer.ts at runtime using TypeScript compiler to access trySendDraft and trySendRichMessage</fact>
    <fact>Plain mode draft with empty text sends undefined as text parameter, triggering "Thinking..." placeholder on Telegram side</fact>
    <fact>Rich mode draft sends markdown content via rich_message.markdown parameter instead of plain text parameter</fact>
    <fact>CRLF sequences in rich draft content are normalized to LF before sending</fact>
    <fact>trySendDraft returns false when required API methods (sendMessageDraft/sendRichMessageDraft) are unavailable, enabling fallback behavior</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The smoke test for draft streaming validates core behaviors of the trySendDraft mechanism. It uses runtime TypeScript transpilation to import and test the actual implementation from telegram-rich-renderer.ts. Tests cover both plain and rich draft modes, verify that empty text triggers the "Thinking..." placeholder (by sending undefined), confirm CRLF normalization for rich content, and validate graceful degradation when API methods are missing. This test suite provides baseline coverage for draft sending correctness but does not cover concurrency scenarios like the TOCTOU race condition identified in the editNow mechanism.</narrative>

    <concept>pattern</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
  </concepts>

If pausedByBudget → bodyFinal += \\\"⏸ Goal paused\\\", no continuation\\n//        c. Else → continuationPlan set for next turn\\n//\\n// We also simulate a multi-turn session: set goal → reply 1 (no done) →\\n// continuation plan generated; run a \\\"continuation turn\\\" → reply 2 (done)\\n// → achievement. Plus budget-exhausted scenario.\\n//\\n// Run: node scripts/check-ab7-goal-loop.mjs\\n\\nimport { mkdir, rm } from \\\"node:fs/promises\\\";\\nimport path from \\\"node:path\\\";\\nimport { fileURLToPath } from \\\"node:url\\\";\\nimport { transform } from \\\"../dist/observerTransformer.js\\\";\\nimport * as goalStore from \\\"../dist/goalStore.js\\\";\\n\\nconst __dirname = path.dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));\\nconst tmpRoot = path.join(__dirname, \\\"..\\\", \\\"tmp\\\", \\\"check-ab7-goal-loop\\\");\\nconst memoryDir = path.join(tmpRoot, \\\"memory\\\");\\n\\nasync function fresh() {\\n  await rm(tmpRoot, { recursive: true, force: true });\\n  await mkdir(memoryDir, { recursive: true });\\n}\\n\\nconst results = [];\\nfunction ok(name) { results.push({ name, pass: true }); console.log(`[PASS] ${name}`); }\\nfunction fail(name, detail) { results.push({ name, pass: false, detail }); console.error(`[FAIL] ${name}: ${detail}`); }\\n\\n/** Mirror of the runPrompt post-reply goal flow, minus Telegram.\\n * Returns what the body additions + continuation plan would look like. */\\nasync function simulateTurn(chatId, replyBuffer, { isContinuation = false } = {}) {\\n  const ab7 = transform(replyBuffer);\\n\\n  let goalAchievementLine = null;\\n  let continuationPlan = null;\\n\\n  if (ab7.goalDone) {\\n    const active = await goalStore.isActive(memoryDir, chatId);\\n    if (active) {\\n      const s = await goalStore.markDone(memoryDir, chatId, ab7.goalDone.reason);\\n      goalAchievementLine = `\\\\n\\\\n✓ Goal achieved: ${s.last_reason}`;\\n    } else {\\n      // fail-open: strip, warn, no state change (transformer already stripped)\\n    }\\n  } else if (!isContinuation) {\\n    const gs = await goalStore.get(memoryDir, chatId);\\n    if (gs && gs.status === \\\"active\\\") {\\n      const result = await goalStore.incrementTurn(\\n        memoryDir, chatId,\\n        replyBuffer ? replyBuffer.slice(0, 200) : undefined,\\n      );\\n      if (result.pausedByBudget) {\\n        goalAchievementLine =\\n          `\\\\n\\\\n⏸ Goal paused — ${result.state.turns_used}/${result.state.max_turns} turns used.`;\\n      } else {\\n        continuationPlan = {\\n          turnIndex: result.state.turns_used + 1,\\n          maxTurns: result.state.max_turns,\\n          objective: result.state.objective,\\n          lastReason: result.state.last_reason,\\n        };\\n      }\\n    }\\n  } else {\\n    // Continuation turn: same incrementTurn logic, just with isContinuation flag\\n    const gs = await goalStore.get(memoryDir, chatId);\\n    if (gs && gs.status === \\\"active\\\") {\\n      const result = await goalStore.incrementTurn(\\n        memoryDir, chatId,\\n        replyBuffer ? replyBuffer.slice(0, 200) : undefined,\\n      );\\n      if (result.pausedByBudget) {\\n        goalAchievementLine =\\n          `\\\\n\\\\n⏸ Goal paused — ${result.state.turns_used}/${result.state.max_turns} turns used.`;\\n      } else {\\n        continuationPlan = {\\n          turnIndex: result.state.turns_used + 1,\\n          maxTurns: result.state.max_turns,\\n          objective: result.state.objective,\\n          lastReason: result.state.last_reason,\\n        };\\n      }\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  return {\\n    displayText: ab7.text,\\n    skillUsages: ab7.skillUsages,\\n    goalDone: ab7.goalDone,\\n    goalAchievementLine,\\n    continuationPlan,\\n  };\\n}\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// 1. No active goal → any reply passes through, no plan\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\nawait fresh();\\n{\\n  const out = await simulateTurn(1001, \\\"plain reply, no tokens\\\");\\n  if (!out.goalAchievementLine && !out.continuationPlan &&\\n      out.displayText === \\\"plain reply, no tokens\\\") {\\n    ok(\\\"1. no active goal → no plan\\\");\\n  } else {\\n    fail(\\\"1. no active goal → no plan\\\", JSON.stringify(out));\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// 2. Active goal, no GOAL_DONE, budget > 1 → continuation planned\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\",\"stderr\":\"\",\"interrupted\":false,\"isImage\":false,\"noOutputExpected\":false}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

    <fact>simulateTurn function mirrors three-stage decision flow: detect GOAL_DONE tokens, mark achievement if done, or increment turn and plan continuation if active</fact>
    <fact>When goalDone detected and goal is active, system appends "✓ Goal achieved: [reason]" line to response body</fact>
    <fact>When goal active but not done and budget allows, system generates continuationPlan with turnIndex, maxTurns, objective, and lastReason for next turn</fact>
    <fact>When turn budget exhausted (pausedByBudget), system appends "⏸ Goal paused — X/Y turns used" line and skips continuation plan generation</fact>
    <fact>Test covers multi-turn scenario: set goal → reply 1 without done marker → continuation plan → reply 2 with done marker → achievement</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The AB-7 goal-loop smoke test validates the goal continuation mechanism by simulating the runPrompt post-reply flow in isolation. The simulateTurn function extracts the core decision logic: parse reply buffer for GOAL_DONE markers using the observer transformer, mark goals as achieved when done, or increment turn count and generate continuation plans when still active. This allows testing the multi-turn goal orchestration without the complexity of a live Telegram connection. The test covers the full lifecycle including budget exhaustion scenarios where goals are paused rather than continued.</narrative>

Extracted from index.ts (P3 split).\\n */\\nimport path from \\\"node:path\\\";\\nimport { readFileSync, writeFileSync, existsSync } from \\\"node:fs\\\";\\nimport { Bot, Context, InlineKeyboard } from \\\"grammy\\\";\\nimport { config } from \\\"./config.js\\\";\\nimport { SessionManager, ChatSession, isRelayDelegatePlaceholderGoal, toolPrefixLines } from \\\"./sessionManager.js\\\";\\nimport {\\n  transform as transformReplyTokens,\\n} from \\\"./observerTransformer.js\\\";\\nimport * as goalStore from \\\"./goalStore.js\\\";\\nimport * as usageStore from \\\"./usageStore.js\\\";\\nimport \\\"./relay.js\\\";\\nimport type { DelegatePayload, ResultPayload } from \\\"./relay.js\\\";\\nimport { isEmbedModelReady, embedText, cosineSim } from \\\"./embed-router.js\\\";\\nimport { detectSkillDir } from \\\"./memory.js\\\";\\nimport { isLocalSpecialist, dispatchToSpecialist, dispatchParallel, startProxy, endProxy, promptProxy, getProxyStats, buildProxySummary } from \\\"./specialist.js\\\";\\nimport type { ParallelTask } from \\\"./specialist.js\\\";\\nimport { appendRoutingLog } from \\\"./routing-log.js\\\";\\nimport { verifySpecialistOutput, buildRetryPrompt } from \\\"./verifier.js\\\";\\nimport { synthesizeDualPath, buildFusionContext } from \\\"./self-fusion.js\\\";\\nimport { saveArtifact } from \\\"./artifact.js\\\";\\nimport { isDuplicateResult } from \\\"./reply-dedup.js\\\";\\nimport { petState, petEmit } from \\\"./pet-server.js\\\";\\nimport { registerTask, completeTask } from \\\"./status-store.js\\\";\\nimport {\\n  maskCodeRegions, buildAskKeyboard, sendFilesByPaths,\\n  splitForTelegram, renderReply, renderDraftReply, askRegistry, TG_MAX,\\n  type ScheduleParsers,\\n} from \\\"./telegram-ui.js\\\";\\nimport {\\n  extractMemoryTokensFromProxy, stripMemoryTokens, saveDelegationContext,\\n  loadDelegationContext, notifyRelayOwner, getRelayLoopGuard, upsertRelayTask,\\n  addRelayTaskOutput,\\n} from \\\"./commands/relay.js\\\";\\nimport { scheduleParsers, parseDuration, parseAbsoluteTime } from \\\"./commands/schedule.js\\\";\\nimport { estimateTokens, pct } from \\\"./commands/misc.js\\\";\\nimport { formatRelative } from \\\"./utils.js\\\";\\nimport { autoWikilink } from \\\"./wiki-retrieval.js\\\";\\nimport { evaluateForAutoSave, recordAutoSaveCandidate } from \\\"./query-auto-save.js\\\";\\nimport { parseRecurrence, computeNextRunAt, formatRecurrence } from \\\"./recurrence.js\\\";\\nimport { ScheduleStore } from \\\"./schedule-store.js\\\";\\nimport { Scheduler } from \\\"./scheduler.js\\\";\\nimport type { ContinuationContext } from \\\"./types.js\\\";\\nimport { tryEditRichMessageDraft, trySendDraft, trySendRichMessage } from \\\"./telegram-rich-renderer.js\\\";\\nimport { quotaHint } from \\\"./provider/quota-error.js\\\";\\n\\n// ─── Types ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/** Same shape as ContinuationContext; re-aliased so the runPrompt body\\n * can distinguish \\\"plan scheduled for next turn\\\" from \\\"we're currently\\n * running one\\\" at the type level. */\\ntype ContinuationPlan = ContinuationContext;\\n\\nexport type RunPromptMode =\\n  | { kind: \\\"user\\\"; replyHeader?: string }\\n  | { kind: \\\"continuation\\\"; context: ContinuationContext }\\n  | { kind: \\\"meta\\\"; silent?: boolean }\\n  | { kind: \\\"relay\\\"; replyTo: string };\\n\\n// ─── Sticker auto-selection ──────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nconst EMOTION_REPRS: Record<string, string> = {\\n  hello: \\\"打招呼 你好 嗨 早安 晚安 greeting\\\",\\n  happy: \\\"開心 太好了 成功 完成了 恭喜 great\\\",\\n  thanks: \\\"謝謝 感謝 thank you 不客氣\\\",\\n  sorry: \\\"抱歉 對不起 不好意思 sorry\\\",\\n  agree: \\\"同意 沒問題 好的 了解 ok sure\\\",\\n  good: \\\"做得好 讚 很棒 nice well done\\\",\\n  clap: \\\"恭喜 太厲害了 完美 bravo\\\",\\n  love: \\\"喜歡 愛 太棒了 超讚\\\",\\n  sad: \\\"可惜 遺憾 不幸 unfortunately\\\",\\n  shock: \\\"驚訝 天啊 沒想到 wow unexpected\\\",\\n  ok: \\\"收到 了解 知道了 acknowledged\\\",\\n};\\nconst STICKER_AUTO_THRESHOLD = 0.65;\\nconst STICKER_AUTO_MAX_REPLY_LEN = 150;\\nlet emotionVectors: Map<string, Float32Array> | null = null;\\n\\nexport async function autoSelectSticker(reply: string): Promise<string | null> {\\n  if (reply.length > STICKER_AUTO_MAX_REPLY_LEN || reply.length < 5) return null;\\n  if (!emotionVectors) {\\n    emotionVectors = new Map();\\n    for (const [emotion, repr] of Object.entries(EMOTION_REPRS)) {\\n      const vec = await embedText(repr);\\n      if (vec) emotionVectors.set(emotion, vec);\\n    }\\n  }\\n  const queryVec = await embedText(reply);\\n  if (!queryVec) return null;\\n  let best = \\\"\\\";\\n  let bestScore = 0;\\n  for (const [emotion, vec] of emotionVectors) {\\n    const score = cosineSim(queryVec, vec);\\n    if (score > bestScore) { bestScore = score; best = emotion; }\\n  }\\n  return bestScore >= STICKER_AUTO_THRESHOLD ? best : null;\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Continuation timer ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nconst GOAL_CONTINUATION_DELAY_MS = 500;\\nconst pendingContinuations = new Map<number, NodeJS.Timeout>();\\n\\nexport function cancelPendingContinuation(chatId: number): void {\\n  const h = pendingContinuations.get(chatId);\\n  if (h) {\\n    clearTimeout(h);\\n    pendingContinuations.delete(chatId);\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Deps interface — mutable state that lives in index.ts ───────────────────\\n\\nexport interface RunPromptDeps {\\n  bot: Bot;\\n  sessions: SessionManager;\\n  rateLimitedUntil: () => number;\\n  onRateLimitRecovery: (fn: () => void | Promise<void>) => () => void;\\n  queuePendingMessage: (chatId: number, text: string) => void;\\n  handleRestart: (ctx: Context, opts?: { reason?: string }) => Promise<void>;\\n  STREAM_EDIT_INTERVAL_MS: number;\\n  MEMORY_DIR: string;\\n  scheduler: Scheduler;\\n  scheduleStore: ScheduleStore;\\n}\\n\\nlet _deps: RunPromptDeps | null = null;\\n\\nexport function initRunPrompt(deps: RunPromptDeps): void {\\n  _deps = deps;\\n}\\n\\nfunction deps(): RunPromptDeps {\\n  if (!_deps) throw new Error(\\\"runPrompt not initialized — call initRunPrompt first\\\");\\n  return _deps;\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Auto-wikilink post-processing ───────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/** After a wiki page is written, scan it and inject [[wikilinks]] for known entities. */\\nfunction applyAutoWikilink(slug: string): void {\\n  const { MEMORY_DIR } = deps();\\n  for (const sub of [\\\"queries\\\", \\\"concepts\\\", \\\"references\\\", \\\"lessons\\\", \\\"tasks\\\"]) {\\n    const filePath = path.join(MEMORY_DIR, \\\"wiki\\\", sub, `${slug}.md`);\\n    if (existsSync(filePath)) {\\n      try {\\n        const content = readFileSync(filePath, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n        const linked = autoWikilink(content);\\n        if (linked !== content) {\\n          writeFileSync(filePath, linked, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n          console.log(`[auto-wikilink] injected links into wiki/${sub}/${slug}.md`);\\n        }\\n      } catch (err) {\\n        console.error(`[auto-wikilink] failed for ${slug}:`, err);\\n      }\\n      return;\\n    }\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// ─── Orphan turn flusher ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n//\\n// Delivers agent output that arrived with NO active prompt turn — e.g. a\\n// Claude Code harness background sub-agent completes and re-invokes the agent\\n// via task-notification, producing an agent_message_chunk while runPrompt's\\n// request→response flush path isn't listening (see SPEC-orphan-turn-flusher).\\n//\\n// SessionManager buffers + debounces those chunks and calls this on quiescence\\n// (wired via sessions.onOrphanTurn in index.ts). This reuses the SAME reply\\n// transform (transformReplyTokens) + render/split helpers as the normal turn —\\n// there is deliberately NO second transform. Tokens that only make sense inside\\n// a live turn (GOAL_DONE / RESTART / CONTINUE / WIKI_QUERY / SKILL_PROPOSE /\\n// SPECIALIST_PROXY / relay*) are intentionally ignored here; user-facing tokens\\n// (text / SEND_FILE / ASK / SCHEDULE / STICKER) are delivered.\\nexport async function flushOrphanTurn(session: ChatSession, rawText: string): Promise<void> {\\n  const { bot, rateLimitedUntil, queuePendingMessage, scheduler, scheduleStore } = deps();\\n  const chatId = session.chatId;\\n\\n  // Dedup: skip content already delivered (e.g. same summary that also came\\n  // through a normal turn, or a duplicate orphan flush). Shares the 5-min\\n  // window used by specialist/parallel result injection.\\n  if (isDuplicateResult(chatId, rawText)) {\\n    console.log(`[orphan ${chatId}] skipped (dedup hit)`);\\n    return;\\n  }\\n\\n  const ab7 = transformReplyTokens(rawText, {\\n    parseDuration,\\n    parseAbsoluteTime,\\n    parseRecurrence,\\n    computeNextRunAt,\\n  });\\n\\n  // SCHEDULE tokens — register the same way the normal path does.\\n  const scheduleReceipts: string[] = [];\\n  for (const se of ab7.scheduleTokens) {\\n    try {\\n      const entry = await scheduleStore.add({\\n        chatId,\\n        userId: session.userId ?? chatId,\\n        runAt: se.runAt,\\n        prompt: se.prompt,\\n        recurrence: se.recurrence,\\n      });\\n      scheduler.arm(entry);\\n      const delta = formatRelative(se.runAt - Date.now());\\n      const recurTag = se.recurrence ? ` [${formatRecurrence(se.recurrence)}]` : \\\"\\\";\\n      scheduleReceipts.push(\\n        `⏰ 已排程 ${entry.id}${recurTag}，${delta} 後觸發（${new Date(se.runAt).toLocaleString()}）`,\\n      );\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      console.error(`[orphan ${chatId}] schedule token register failed:`, err);\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  // Surface (but don't act on) tokens that require live-turn context.\\n  const ignored: string[] = [];\\n  if (ab7.goalDone) ignored.push(\\\"GOAL_DONE\\\");\\n  if (ab7.restartToken) ignored.push(\\\"RESTART\\\");\\n  if (ab7.continueToken) ignored.push(\\\"CONTINUE\\\");\\n  if (ab7.wikiQuery) ignored.push(\\\"WIKI_QUERY\\\");\\n  if (ab7.skillPropose) ignored.push(\\\"SKILL_PROPOSE\\\");\\n  if (ab7.specialistProxy) ignored.push(\\\"SPECIALIST_PROXY\\\");\\n  if (ab7.moaPlan) ignored.push(\\\"MOA_PLAN\\\");\\n  if (ignored.length) {\\n    console.log(`[orphan ${chatId}] ignoring turn-context token(s): ${ignored.join(\\\", \\\")}`);\\n  }\\n\\n  const body = scheduleReceipts.length\\n    ? (ab7.text ? `${ab7.text}\\\\n\\\\n${scheduleReceipts.join(\\\"\\\\n\\\")}` : scheduleReceipts.join(\\\"\\\\n\\\"))\\n    : ab7.text;\\n  const askKeyboard = buildAskKeyboard(ab7.askTokens);\\n\\n  // Text delivery (chunked). Rate-limit aware: queue for replay if throttled.\\n  if (body.trim().length > 0) {\\n    const chunks = splitForTelegram(body);\\n    for (let i = 0; i < chunks.length; i++) {\\n      const isLast = i === chunks.length - 1;\\n      const pageTag = chunks.length > 1 ? `(${i + 1}/${chunks.length})\\\\n` : \\\"\\\";\\n      const text = pageTag + chunks[i];\\n      const extra = isLast && askKeyboard ? { reply_markup: askKeyboard } : undefined;\\n      if (rateLimitedUntil() > Date.now()) {\\n        queuePendingMessage(chatId, text);\\n        continue;\\n      }\\n      try {\\n        const msg = await bot.api.sendMessage(chatId, text, extra);\\n        if (isLast && askKeyboard && msg?.message_id !== undefined) {\\n          askRegistry.set(msg.message_id, ab7.askTokens);\\n        }\\n      } catch (err) {\\n        console.error(`[orphan ${chatId}] chunk send failed, queueing for replay:`, err);\\n        queuePendingMessage(chatId, text);\\n      }\\n      if (!isLast) await new Promise<void>((r) => setTimeout(r, 1100));\\n    }\\n  } else if (askKeyboard) {\\n    // ASK-only orphan turn (rare) — carry the keyboard on a minimal message.\\n    try {\\n      const msg = await bot.api.sendMessage(chatId, \\\"（背景任務完成）\\\", { reply_markup: askKeyboard });\\n      if (msg?.message_id !== undefined) askRegistry.set(msg.message_id, ab7.askTokens);\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      console.error(`[orphan ${chatId}] ask-only send failed:`, err);\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  // SEND_FILE — sendFilesByPaths needs a Context-like; build a minimal shim.\\n  if (ab7.sendFiles.length > 0) {\\n    const fakeCtx = {\\n      chat: { id: chatId, type: \\\"private\\\" as const },\\n      replyWithPhoto: (f: unknown, extra?: unknown) => bot.api.sendPhoto(chatId, f as any, extra as any),\\n      replyWithDocument: (f: unknown, extra?: unknown) => bot.api.sendDocument(chatId, f as any, extra as any),\\n    } as unknown as Context;\\n    try {\\n      await sendFilesByPaths(fakeCtx, ab7.sendFiles);\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      console.error(`[orphan ${chatId}] sendFilesByPaths failed:`, err);\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  // Stickers\\n  for (const emotion of ab7.stickerEmotions) {\\n    const fileId = STICKER_MAP[emotion];\\n    if (!fileId) continue;\\n    try {\\n      await bot.api.sendSticker(chatId, fileId);\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      console.warn(`[orphan ${chatId}] sticker ${emotion} failed — ${(err as Error).message}`);\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  console.log(\\n    `[orphan ${chatId}] delivered (textChars=${body.length}, files=${ab7.sendFiles.length}, ` +\\n content.thinking:
The user is creating a new utility module for async call coalescing. This is a fix for the root cause that was diagnosed earlier - the TOCTOU race condition in the streaming edit mechanism where multiple concurrent calls to editNow would each create duplicate messages.

Key points:
- New file: src/async-coalesce.ts
- Implements coalesceAsyncRunner function
- Purpose: wrap an async function to ensure at most one in-flight call at a time
- If called while in-flight, mark as pending and return immediately (no queue, no concurrency)
- After completion, if pending flag set, run once more with latest state
- Includes isCancelled check to prevent running after cancellation
- Motivation explicitly documented: fix for 2026-07-11 roadmap incident where editNow was triggered from multiple paths (onUpdate chunks, keepalive, rate-limit recovery) and when draft sending failed, the time gate opened fully, causing concurrent calls in the "draft failed → !placeholder → sendMessage" degradation path to each create a placeholder, resulting in 10+ duplicate messages
- This latch pattern serializes the degradation path, eliminating the TOCTOU race

This is a bugfix implementing the solution to the diagnosed problem.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Async call coalescing utility created</title>
  <subtitle>New utility prevents concurrent execution by serializing async calls with pending flag for latest-state re-runs</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>File src/async-coalesce.ts created with coalesceAsyncRunner function that wraps async functions to ensure at most one in-flight execution</fact>
    <fact>When called during in-flight execution, runner marks pending flag and returns immediately without queuing or concurrent execution</fact>
    <fact>After execution completes, if pending flag set, runner automatically re-runs once with latest state, coalescing intermediate calls</fact>
    <fact>Runner accepts optional isCancelled callback to prevent starting or re-running after cancellation</fact>
    <fact>Utility implements fix for 2026-07-11 roadmap incident where editNow concurrent calls in draft-failure degradation path created 10+ duplicate placeholder messages</fact>
    <fact>Latch pattern eliminates TOCTOU race by serializing the "draft failed → !placeholder → sendMessage" code path where placeholder variable was checked before being set</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Created async-coalesce.ts utility to fix the streaming edit duplicate message bug diagnosed earlier. The coalesceAsyncRunner function wraps an async function to guarantee serial execution: only one call runs at a time, concurrent calls set a pending flag and return immediately, and after completion the runner checks the pending flag to execute once more with the latest state. This coalescing pattern prevents the TOCTOU race condition where editNow was triggered by multiple sources (chunk updates, keepalive, rate-limit recovery) and when draft sending failed, the throttle time gate opened fully, allowing concurrent calls to each execute the degradation path and create separate placeholder messages. The utility includes cancellation support to stop execution cleanly when the turn finalizes.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read></files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>src/async-coalesce.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

content.thinking:
The user is making edits to implement the fix for the duplicate message bug. They're renaming `editNow` to `editNowInner` as the first step of wrapping it with the async coalescing pattern they just created.

This is the beginning of implementing the fix to prevent concurrent execution of the editNow function that was causing the TOCTOU race condition.

<observation>
  <type>refactor</type>
  <title>Streaming edit function renamed to prepare for coalescing wrapper</title>
  <subtitle>editNow renamed to editNowInner as first step of wrapping with concurrency control</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Function editNow in src/run-prompt.ts renamed to editNowInner at line 445</fact>
    <fact>Rename prepares for wrapping the inner function with coalesceAsyncRunner utility to prevent concurrent execution</fact>
    <fact>editNowInner handles draft mode and placeholder mode streaming edits during agent prompt execution</fact>
    <fact>Function contains draft TTL degradation path where TOCTOU race condition occurs when concurrent calls check !placeholder before it gets assigned</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Renamed the editNow function to editNowInner as the first step in applying the concurrency fix for the duplicate message bug. This prepares the function to be wrapped by the coalesceAsyncRunner utility created earlier. The inner function will remain unchanged in its core logic, but will be invoked through a coalescing wrapper that ensures only one execution runs at a time, preventing the race condition where multiple concurrent calls in the draft-failure degradation path each create separate placeholder messages when checking the !placeholder condition before the variable is assigned.</narrative>
  <concepts>

content.thinking:
The user is continuing to observe edits being made to fix the duplicate message bug. Let me track what's happening:

1. First edit added a `turnFinalized` check in the draft degradation path where it tries to create a placeholder
2. Second edit adds another `turnFinalized` check at the beginning of the placeholder edit path

These are adding guards to prevent late-arriving editNow calls from executing after the turn has already finalized and sent the final message. This addresses the second issue mentioned in the diagnosis - that editNowInner doesn't check turnFinalized and can overwrite the final message with a streaming frame.

These are incremental safety checks being added before wrapping the function with the coalescing runner.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Added turnFinalized guard to placeholder edit path</title>
  <subtitle>Prevents late-arriving streaming edits from overwriting final message after turn completes</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Added turnFinalized check at line 503 in editNowInner function before placeholder edit logic executes</fact>
    <fact>Previous edit already added turnFinalized check in draft degradation path to prevent creating placeholder after finalization</fact>
    <fact>Guards prevent race condition where in-flight editNow calls resolve after turnFinalized flag is set and overwrite final rendered message</fact>
    <fact>Addresses secondary bug identified in diagnosis where editNowInner lacks turnFinalized check unlike recovery listener which has latch protection</fact>
    <fact>Combined with earlier draft degradation guard, ensures editNowInner exits early at two critical points when turn has already completed</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Added turnFinalized guard at the beginning of the placeholder edit code path in editNowInner function. This prevents late-arriving streaming edit calls that are still in-flight when the turn finalizes from executing placeholder edits and overwriting the final message. Combined with the earlier guard added to the draft degradation path, this ensures that editNowInner stops execution early if the turn has already finalized, preventing both unwanted placeholder creation and final message overwrites. These guards address a secondary issue identified in the diagnosis beyond the primary TOCTOU race condition - that editNowInner lacks the turnFinalized check that protects other code paths from executing after turn completion.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read></files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>src/run-prompt.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

content.thinking:
This is the key edit that wraps the editNowInner function with the coalesceAsyncRunner utility. Let me understand what's happening:

1. They've already created the async-coalesce.ts utility
2. They've renamed editNow to editNowInner
3. They've added turnFinalized checks in two critical places in editNowInner
4. Now they're creating the final editNow by wrapping editNowInner with coalesceAsyncRunner

The comment in Chinese explains:
- onUpdate (every chunk), keepalive, and recovery all trigger editNow from multiple sources
- lastEditAt is only updated after success, so during send failure the time gate is wide open
- Concurrent calls in the "trySendDraft fails → !placeholder → sendMessage" degradation path each create their own placeholder
- This caused the 2026-07-11 roadmap incident with 10+ identical messages
- coalesce ensures at most one in-flight at a time, with intermediate triggers merged into a single re-run after completion

The wrapper uses `() => turnFinalized` as the cancellation check, so once the turn finalizes, the coalescing runner will stop executing.

This is the final piece that completes the fix. The import statement for coalesceAsyncRunner must be missing from the observed edits, but it should be imported from ./async-coalesce.js.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Wrapped editNowInner with coalescing runner to prevent concurrent execution</title>
  <subtitle>Completes duplicate message fix by serializing all editNow calls from multiple trigger sources</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Created final editNow function by wrapping editNowInner with coalesceAsyncRunner at line 525 in src/run-prompt.ts</fact>
    <fact>Coalescing wrapper ensures maximum one in-flight execution at any time, with pending flag to trigger one final re-run with latest state after completion</fact>
    <fact>Wrapper accepts turnFinalized check as cancellation callback to stop execution after turn completes</fact>
    <fact>Comment documents root cause: onUpdate, keepalive, and recovery callbacks all trigger editNow concurrently while lastEditAt only updates after success</fact>
    <fact>During send failures time gate opens fully allowing concurrent calls in draft-failure degradation path to each create separate placeholder messages</fact>
    <fact>Fix resolves 2026-07-11 roadmap incident where 10+ identical messages appeared from concurrent placeholder creation in !placeholder check before assignment</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Completed the duplicate message bug fix by wrapping editNowInner with the coalesceAsyncRunner utility created earlier. The final editNow function now serializes all invocations from multiple trigger sources (onUpdate per chunk, draft keepalive timer, and rate-limit recovery callback). When concurrent calls occur, the coalescer marks a pending flag and returns immediately without queuing or executing. After the current execution completes, if the pending flag is set, one final re-run executes with the latest state, effectively coalescing all intermediate calls. The turnFinalized check passed as the cancellation callback ensures the coalescer stops execution after the turn completes. This eliminates the TOCTOU race condition where concurrent calls in the draft-failure degradation path each checked !placeholder before the variable was assigned, causing each to create a separate placeholder message and resulting in 10+ duplicate messages with identical content.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read></files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>src/run-prompt.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

This is the final piece that ties together the complete fix:

  <title>Added import for coalesceAsyncRunner utility</title>
  <subtitle>Completes bug fix implementation by importing concurrency control utility into run-prompt module</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Added import statement for coalesceAsyncRunner from async-coalesce.js module at line 50 in src/run-prompt.ts</fact>
    <fact>Import statement placed after telegram-rich-renderer imports and before quotaHint import</fact>
    <fact>Import completes the implementation chain: utility created → editNow renamed → guards added → wrapper applied → import added</fact>
    <fact>Fix is now fully integrated and ready for testing to verify duplicate message bug is resolved</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Added the import statement for the coalesceAsyncRunner utility to complete the duplicate message bug fix. With this final piece, the entire fix is now fully implemented: the async-coalesce.ts utility provides the concurrency control mechanism, editNowInner contains the core streaming edit logic with turnFinalized guards at two critical paths (draft degradation and placeholder edit), the editNow wrapper applies coalescing to serialize all concurrent calls, and now the import makes the utility available in the run-prompt module. The fix is complete and ready for runtime testing to confirm that the 2026-07-11 roadmap incident where 10+ duplicate messages appeared is resolved.</narrative>

    <concept>what-changed</concept>
  </concepts>

    <file>src/run-prompt.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

  await sleep(5);\\n    if (!placeholder) {\\n      // 模擬 sendMessage 網路延遲（第二個 await gap — 原事故的 TOCTOU 窗口）\\n      await sleep(10);\\n      messagesSent++;\\n      placeholder = { message_id: messagesSent };\\n    }\\n  };\\n\\n  // 無鎖版本先證明 race 真的存在（10 路並發 → 10 則）\\n  const naiveRuns = Array.from({ length: 10 }, () => body());\\n  await Promise.all(naiveRuns);\\n  assert(\\\"無鎖版本重現事故（10 並發 → 10 則訊息）\\\", messagesSent === 10,\\n    `messagesSent=${messagesSent}`);\\n\\n  // 有鎖版本：同樣 10 路並發 → 只有 1 則\\n  placeholder = undefined;\\n  messagesSent = 0;\\n  const editNow = coalesceAsyncRunner(body);\\n  const runs = Array.from({ length: 10 }, () => editNow());\\n  await Promise.all(runs);\\n  await sleep(40); // 等 trailing 補跑完\\n  assert(\\\"coalesce 版本 10 並發只建 1 則 placeholder\\\", messagesSent === 1,\\n    `messagesSent=${messagesSent}`);\\n}\\n\\n// ─── 2. 並發合併：burst 期間的觸發合併成「1 次執行 + 1 次補跑」 ───\\n{\\n  let executions = 0;\\n  const editNow = assert(\\\"8 路並發合併為 2 次執行（1 主 + 1 trailing）\\\", executions === 2,\\n    `executions=${executions}`);\\n}\\n\\n// ─── 3. trailing 補跑拿到最新狀態 ───\\n{\\n  let latest = \\\"\\\";\\n  let rendered = [];\\n  const editNow = coalesceAsyncRunner(async () => {\\n    const snapshot = latest;\\n    await sleep(10);\\n    rendered.push(snapshot);\\n  });\\n  latest = \\\"v1\\\";\\n  const p1 = editNow();\\n  latest = \\\"v2\\\";\\n  void editNow(); // in-flight 期間觸發 → pending\\n  latest = \\\"v3\\\";\\n  void editNow(); // 合併進同一個 pending\\n  await p1;\\n  await sleep(30);\\n  assert(\\\"trailing 補跑渲染最新內容（v3）\\\", rendered.length === 2 && rendered[1] === \\\"v3\\\",\\n    JSON.stringify(rendered));\\n}\\n\\n// ─── 4. 取消語意：cancelled 不啟動、不補跑 ───\\n{\\n  let cancelled = false;\\n  let executions = 0;\\n  const editNow = coalesceAsyncRunner(async () => {\\n    executions++;\\n    await sleep(10);\\n  }, () => cancelled);\\n\\n  cancelled = true;\\n  await editNow();\\n  assert(\\\"cancelled 時不啟動\\\", executions === 0);\\n\\n  // 執行中途 finalize → 不補跑\\n  cancelled = false;\\n  const p = editNow();\\n  void editNow(); // pending\\n  cancelled = true; // 模擬 turnFinalized 在 in-flight 期間翻 true\\n  await p;\\n  await sleep(30);\\n  assert(\\\"in-flight 期間 finalize → 不補跑\\\", executions === 1, `executions=${executions}`);\\n}\\n\\n// ─── 5. 序列呼叫每次都執行（不誤傷正常節流路徑） ───\\n{\\n  let executions = 0;\\n  const editNow = coalesceAsyncRunner(async () => {\\n    executions++;\\n  });\\n  await editNow();\\n  await editNow();\\n  await editNow();\\n  assert(\\\"序列呼叫每次執行\\\", executions === 3, `executions=${executions}`);\\n}\\n\\nconsole.log(`\\\\n${passed}/${passed} passed`);\\n\"}"</parameters>

1. The test recreates the actual incident - the TOCTOU race condition where 10 concurrent calls create 10 messages without locking, but only 1 message with the coalescing runner
2. Tests that concurrent calls are properly coalesced (8 calls → 2 executions: 1 main + 1 trailing)

<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Created smoke test for editNow concurrency fix</title>
  <subtitle>Test validates coalescing behavior and recreates original incident to prove fix works</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Created test file scripts/check-editnow-race.mjs that validates coalesceAsyncRunner behavior through 5 test scenarios</fact>
    <fact>Test #1 recreates 2026-07-11 roadmap incident showing 10 concurrent calls create 10 messages without lock but only 1 message with coalescing wrapper</fact>
    <fact>Test #2 validates concurrent burst merging where 8 parallel calls execute only twice: 1 main execution plus 1 trailing re-run</fact>
    <fact>Test #3 confirms trailing re-run captures latest state by verifying v3 rendered after v1/v2 updates during in-flight execution</fact>
    <fact>Test #4 validates cancellation semantics where cancelled flag prevents both initial start and trailing re-run when turnFinalized during execution</fact>
    <fact>Test #5 ensures serial calls each execute to confirm coalescing doesn't break normal throttling behavior</fact>
    <fact>Test transpiles async-coalesce.ts at runtime using TypeScript compiler to import and validate actual implementation</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Created comprehensive smoke test for the editNow concurrency fix. The test recreates the exact TOCTOU race condition from the 2026-07-11 incident: it simulates the draft-failure degradation path where trySendDraft fails (first await gap) then checks !placeholder before sendMessage (second await gap creating the race window). Without locking, 10 concurrent calls each see !placeholder as true and create 10 separate messages. With the coalescing wrapper, the same 10 concurrent calls result in only 1 message. Additional tests validate the coalescing semantics: burst calls merge into 1 execution + 1 trailing re-run, the trailing run captures the latest state, cancellation prevents both start and re-run, and serial calls still execute normally. The test provides proof that the fix resolves the original incident.</narrative>

  <parameters>"{\"command\":\"cd \\\"G:/AI/telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\" && git add src/async-coalesce.ts src/run-prompt.ts scripts/check-editnow-race.mjs && git commit -m \\\"fix(streaming): editNow 加並發互斥，修復 draft 降級 race 狂發相同訊息\\n\\n事故（2026-07-11 roadmap turn）：agent 單一回答，Telegram 卻出現 1x 則\\n相同內容訊息。根因是 editNow 無 in-flight 鎖：onUpdate 每 chunk\\nfire-and-forget 觸發，而 lastEditAt 只在成功後更新——draft 送出一旦開始\\n失敗，節流閘門對每個 chunk 全開（~10 呼叫/秒），並發呼叫在\\n「trySendDraft 失敗 → !placeholder → await sendMessage」的 TOCTOU 窗口\\n內各自建立 placeholder，1-2 秒窗口 ≈ 10-15 則重複訊息。\\n\\n修法：\\n- 新增 src/async-coalesce.ts coalesceAsyncRunner：同一時間最多一個\\n  in-flight，中間觸發合併成結束後補跑一次（拿最新 buffer）\\n- editNow 以 coalescer 包裝，並以 turnFinalized 為取消條件（連帶補上\\n  「in-flight 幀在 final render 後 resolve 再冒一則」的第二個洞）\\n- 降級分支與 placeholder edit 前加 turnFinalized 再檢查（await 後狀態\\n  可能已翻轉）\\n\\n同類掃描：index.ts / relay.ts 的 proxy editNow 無降級建訊分支且內容比對\\n在 await 前同步執行，無同類風險，不動。\\n\\n驗證：check-editnow-race 7/7（含無鎖版重現 10 並發 → 10 則）、\\ncheck-draft-streaming、check-draft-render、check-ab7-goal-loop 全過，\\ntsc -p . 過。\\n\\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>\\nClaude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_015NRnSHa9WmgC92LR1K9k27\\\" && git log --oneline -1\",\"description\":\"Commit editNow race fix\"}"</parameters>
